fan_analyst.note: Hittin' Season and The Phillies Show are in near-total alarm — nine-game losing streak called historically embarrassing, front office indicted for roster construction failures, Thomson's job openly threatened; small coping notes about wild-card era and eventual rebound keep score from bottoming out entirely.

radio_populist.note: Talk-radio host expresses near-complete despair — cannot envision another win, calls the situation a 'disaster,' openly predicts managerial and coaching firings, questions whether star players still have competitive fire.

themes.name: Nine Games of Freefall
themes.sample: A nine-game losing streak — tied for the longest this century — has produced the worst record in the National League, a -50 run differential worst in baseball by 15 runs, and a sweep out of Wrigley for the first time since 1973.

themes.name: Offense in Historic Collapse
themes.sample: The lineup went 0-for-26 with runners in scoring position over a six-game stretch, averages 2 runs per game during the losing streak, and has yet to beat a left-handed starter all season — with pitch recognition and passive zone coverage cited as root causes.

themes.name: Front Office on Trial
themes.sample: A $300 million payroll forced to deploy a rookie in his second career start as cleanup hitter is framed as an indictment of the offseason — no real cleanup bat acquired, $35 million paid to two players not on the roster, and organizational failure to develop star bats dating back decades.

themes.name: Thomson's Job in the Balance
themes.sample: Despite broad personal respect for the manager, there is open acknowledgment that if the losing continues through late May, a change becomes inevitable — with the parallel to Girardi's dismissal drawn explicitly and the path of least resistance pointed squarely at the dugout.

themes.name: Injury Pile-Up Deepens the Crisis
themes.sample: J.T. Realmuto on the IL with back spasms, Alvarado and Duran both unavailable in the bullpen, and Taiwan Walker released — leaving a rotation without a single seven-inning start in 25 games and a catching tandem of Marchand and Stubbs that inspires little confidence.

quotes.text: This is a freaking disaster. They are actually playing like the worst team in baseball.
It is shocking and it's like it's gross. How is this possibly happening to a team like the Phillies?
quotes.source_hint: fan analyst (Phillies Talk / NBC Sports Philadelphia)

quotes.text: Making Felix Reyes your No. 4 hitter is an indictment on Dave Dombrowski.
I'm not sure they have the fire anymore. I'm really not.

reasoning: A nine-game losing streak, the worst record in the National League, a historically bad run differential, and a 0-for-26 stretch with runners in scoring position have pushed fan mood to near-absolute lows across every dimension. The sole divergence in tone is between apocalyptic alarm — forecasting managerial dismissal, questioning whether star players retain competitive hunger, and declaring the front office's roster construction a failure — and a narrower thread of reluctant, conditional optimism that the wild-card era keeps the postseason mathematically alive and that this roster is too talented for the collapse to be permanent. That conditional optimism does not constitute expressed belief in a deep run; it is structural reassurance offered against a backdrop of genuine despair, and postseason belief is scored accordingly.

podcasts_used.title: Phillies in a freefall as April struggles continue, losing streak hits nine games
Where Is the Bottom For the Phillies?
Rob Thomson is on the hot seat
